entry.title: Re: How to show Ramp-Up Coverage in SAP
entry.content:
Hi @Piteross1_ ,
Managing a ramp‑up plan in SAP—especially when the business refuses to reduce demand—can feel like trying to steer a ship with two captains giving opposite orders. The good news is that SAP gives you several ways to show reality without changing the official demand, which is exactly what you need.

I think, to Present a Realistic Ramp‑Up Plan Without Changing Demand in SAP
Your challenge has two parts:
1. Demand must remain high (management requirement).
2. You need to show what is actually achievable based on contracts, POs, and realistic supply.

SAP cannot magically reconcile these two, but you can use SAP’s planning tools to show the gap between official demand and realistic supply—without altering the demand elements. Here are the most effective approaches.

Use Stock/Requirements List (MD04) or Product View (RRP3) to Show Coverage
You can show management the coverage profile based on:

  • Current stock
  • Firmed purchase orders
  • Firmed production orders
  • Contracts / scheduling agreements
  • Supplier lead times

You will present it In RRP3, switch to the Pegging / Receipts & Requirements view and export:

  • Total demand per week/month
  • Total confirmed supply
  • Resulting coverage (days or weeks)
  • Shortages by period

This gives a factual, SAP‑generated picture of what the system can support.

entry.title: Re: Control Key YBM1 YBM2 S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
1. Why You See Two PR Lines
In Public Cloud, when you flag an operation with YBM2 (external processing), the system automatically generates a purchase requisition line for the operation itself. If you also plan services (expected value / overall limit) under that operation, the system creates a second PR line for the service.
This is by design: SAP treats the external operation as a “framework” requirement (the vendor activity) and the service line as the detailed procurement object. That’s why you see both.

2. Is This Standard Behavior?
Yes. In Public Cloud, two PR lines are expected when you combine YBM2 + service planning. The “operation PR line” represents the external activity. The “service PR line” represents the actual service procurement (with limits, expected value, etc.). This is consistent with the way the Manage Maintenance Orders app integrates with Purchase Requisitions.

3. Best Practice for External Services with Limits
Use YBM2 to mark the operation as external. Enter the service lines with expected value and overall limit. The PR line for the service is the one that will drive the PO and vendor billing. The PR line for the operation is informational and ensures the order structure is consistent. To connect a vendor contract/agreement number, maintain the source of supply (contract or supplier) in the PR line for the service. This way, the PO references the contract, and the operation PR line does not interfere.

4. Can You Suppress the Operation PR Line?
In Public Cloud, suppression is not supported. The external operation PR line is always created when YBM2 is used. In on‑premise, you could customize this behavior, but in Public Cloud the design is fixed. The recommended approach is to ignore the operation PR line for procurement purposes and work only with the service PR line (where you maintain limits and contract references).

entry.title: Re: RAP Action Updates Database, But Fiori UI Doesn’t Refresh Automatically – Requires Manual "
entry.content: If your BO is draft enabled and you are using Fiori elements you can try with RAP side effects: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/side-effects?version=sap_btp

entry.title: Automation around Transports (STMS_IMPORT)
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I am wondering whether somebody from community has "good" experience with automation transport requests in SAP. I am talking about technical transports requests (STMS), not logistics.

In the past I build a process automation with a form where selected users could enter a transport request. The bot checked that request for some preconditions and imported it then into a SAP system.

In the meantime and with much more experience I doubt whether this scenario is really good for a "bot". The check of the preconditions is really complex, because the request is only allowed to have special objects included and nothing else. I am importing with BAPI. There I only can start the import but don't get a reliable feedback (especially for huge requests). Also there are no BAPI for STMS_QA, so I don't get this list clean.

I also noticed that SAP itself doesn't have any automation around STMS_IMPORT in her portfolio (store). That must also have some reasons because there are over 600 projects included and STMS uses nearly every customer.

In the past we dealt that topic with tickets and a manual import from "IT guys".

What are your thoughts about that issue?

entry.title: Re: BOM Status with Change Number
entry.content:
Dear Chris,

Many Thanks for your clear explanation, but I still can't create a production order with BOM Status3....

I have a Change Number adapted for BOM's:

My BOM (Status 3) was created with this valid (02/02/2026) Change Number:

No issues with the Production Version:

 

But in CO01, when selecting it, the production version:

I still got the error:  "No valid production version found for material 5820-40030000
Message no. C2142"

Now, going back to CS02, If I change the BOM to Status "1", I can create my order…

What am I missing?

Thanks! KR,

Nuno

entry.title: Re: Control Key YBM1 YBM2 S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Thank you Lakshmipathi for your structured response.
in the mean time i have done some more digging and understand that this is by design.

My question after that is : Why...
- Why does SAP want to create a seperate PR for something that we do not create a PO for?
- what is the usecase of that PR?
- it clutters the inbox of the buyer/purchaser

is there something holding us back from not using YBM2 and just use YBM1? if we consistently use P60-A0 + YBM1 + service limits, what is the issue in that?
will this prevent us from automation processees, financial insights or any other overviews?
i have asked this question in most disciplines in our org, but can't see the benefit of the YBM2 key, apart from the word "external service" stated somewhere. 
We used to devide external operations purely by using workcenter P60-A0.

looking forward to your insight.

kind regards,

Nick

entry.title: Re: 🇧🇷 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Rio de Janeiro, Brazil)
entry.content: Será um dia de muito aprendizado. Boraaaaaa!!!!!

entry.title: Re: Max/Recommended number of SKU´s for RDP
entry.content:
There are no documented limits for the number of products and the system scales well even for large number of products.

Obviously calculation times for report data will increase with the increased amount of products, but remain within reasonable limits.

As most customers replicate the data during the night, the recalculation times are not a concern.

Some practical tips that your client can consider:

  • Do not replicate obsolete products, which are not used anymore (possibly the cross-plant status can be used for filtering out)
  • Do not replicate products, which will definitely be out of scope for the reports (e.g a product designed for specific market, where the market is not in the project scope)

entry.title: Re: In SuccessFactors LMS, I need to list an instructor's...

If you`re not interested in take the Instructor list, maybe this could be helpful

USER
GET /learning/odatav4/public/user/scheduledoffering-service/v1/EnrollmentListings?$filter=criteria/scheduleID eq 1234
ADMIN
GET /learning/odatav4/public/admin/scheduledoffering-service/v1/EnrollmentListings?$filter=criteria/scheduleID eq 1234

Regards

entry.title: Not able to see details in SAP ME for Customer Success dsahboard
entry.content:
How can we access customer Success (Renewal, Upcoming Renewal) tabs and even if requesting for authorization.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Not able to see details in SAP ME for Customer Succes...
entry.content:
S-user does not have sufficient authorizations. The following authorizations are missing from the S-user:

  • the Partner - End Customer Sales & Adoption
  • the Partner - End Customer Info & Support

S-user needs to get the missing authorizations from SAP Partner Portal.

SAP Support does not support any topics or issues on the SAP Customer Success, SAP Partner Portal, end customer support, or the Partner Dashboards in SAP for Me.

Who to Contact?

  • Each SAP Partner has a Partner Experience Manager (dedicated SAP Contact) who can be contacted.
  • If you don't know who your Partner Experience Manager is, please contact SAP Partner Support using the "Get Support" on the partner "Get assistance" dashboard.
    Please Note- They do not work within the case application or via Expert Chat 

For the most current contact options for SAP Partner Support and the helpline, go to http://partneredge.sap.com and click? in the upper right corner (direct link https://partneredge.sap.com/en/public/get-help.html) or use the Get Help on the side of the portal

IMPORTANT!
SAP Support cannot resolve the topics listed in the Symptom section via case/chat and cannot assist with partner-related questions. SAP Support does not have access to the e-mail communication between a customer and Partner Support and cannot contact them for you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud, on AVC Need to derive BOM quantity...
entry.content:
Good day @Lord_Reaver 

Thank you for your question. See below.

Question: Is it technically supported in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to dynamically derive the BOM component quantity from a configurable characteristic (numeric, continuous value)?

Answer: Generally, yes, but the supported pattern is not to directly assign a formula key with a procedure such as ROMS1 = $ROOT.Z_LONGITUD.

In Public Cloud, item category R is supported in BOMs, and variable size item quantity is calculated with maintained dimensions plus formula.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/8b76540d4fae4d119b9d74fe1c44a696.html?locale=en-US

Question: What is the correct modelling approach?

Answer: The usual modelling approach is:

  1. To use BOM item category R for the cable component
  2. Then maintain the size dimension length in M
  3. Maintain a quantity formula
  4. Map the configuration characteristic value (for example Z_LONGITUD) to the variable size item dimension using a reference characteristic or supported dependency target
  5. Let BOM explosion calculate the quantity and pass it to MRP.

The key point is that the formula key is part of the BOM quantity calculation setup, not a characteristic variable that you write to directly in a procedure.

Question: Should this be done via Variable Size Item reference characteristics?

Answer: Yes, that is the right direction assuming your tenant exposes the needed AVC/BOM mapping options. You normally map the configurable value to the variable size input dimension, and then the formula calculates the quantity.

Question: Is Quantity Formula supported?

Answer: Yes, for variable size items this is standard BOM functionality.

If you get a syntax error with ROMS1 = $ROOT.Z_LONGITUD, check whether ROMS1 is a formula key, the dependency should instead assign to a supported dimension target, and the dependency is attached at the correct object.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Colombia Production Number Range Retrieval from DIAN
entry.content:
Hi @naveen_kolli,

Congratulations on your post! Very clear and helpful.

I have a situation with a client in Colombia and I would greatly appreciate it if you could contribute your opinion.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/afaad59f2f68479aa87fc13aae85c231/8634cab34a2a4ed192f5d0eb7b61afb3.html?version=2023.latest

Sincerely,

entry.title: Unable to generate Maintenance Certificate
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,
We are trying to renew the maintenance certificate for the system; however, we are unable to complete the renewal on the SAP Service Marketplace.

The system is displaying the following error message:
Error: 8+ (Request renew Maintenance not possible: Error: 😎

Due to this issue, the maintenance certificate renewal cannot be completed.

Could you please investigate this at your earliest convenience and advise on the necessary steps to resolve the issue?
Please let us know if you require any additional information from our side.

Thank you for your support.


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

me.sap.com/licensekeys

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

After a couple of hours waiting, you should be able to renew the certificates.

Thanks for your patience ad fr learning with SAP!

entry.title: Społeczności z powiatu SAPowiskiego spotyka poraz pierwszy w 2026 roku!
entry.content:
Nowe osoby w grupie

Dołączyło do nas kilka nowych osób, które wniosły świeżą perspektywę i sporo ciekawych pytań dotyczących rozwiązań AI oraz ich zastosowania w realnych projektach. Cieszymy się, że jesteście z nami. Liczymy, że poczujecie się tu jak w transakcji, która wreszcie działa – czyli tak, jakby ktoś włączył debuger i wyłączył wszystkie niepotrzebne user-exit’y.

Nowe podejście do rzeczywistości AI

Dyskutowaliśmy o tym, jak dynamicznie zmienia się rynek i w jaki sposób sztuczna inteligencja wchodzi do codziennych procesów: od automatyzacji, przez analizę danych, po nowe modele pracy konsultantów i deweloperów.

Wspólny wniosek był prosty: AI nie jest już dodatkiem do projektów, ale jednym z filarów ich realizacji. To wymusza nowe podejście do nauki, planowania i samego patrzenia na systemy SAP.

Co nowego w świecie SAP

Omówiliśmy m.in.:

  • nowe kursy i aktualizacje ścieżek certyfikacyjnych,

  • materiały przygotowujące do egzaminów SAP z elementami AI,

  • nowości w narzędziach wspierających pracę konsultantów, w tym rozwijane funkcje oparte na modelach generatywnych.

SAP coraz mocniej akcentuje połączenie klasycznego know-how procesowego z kompetencjami AI. To będzie jeden z kluczowych kierunków rozwoju w 2026 roku.

Plany na następne miesiące

Na kolejnym spotkaniu wracamy z jeszcze większą porcją wiedzy i dyskusji. Zapowiadają się sesje techniczne, prezentacje wdrożeniowe oraz przegląd nowinek, które już teraz kształtują przyszłość rynku.

Dziękujemy wszystkim za udział i zaangażowanie. Ten rok dopiero się zaczyna, a my już widzimy, że będzie bardzo ciekawy. Jeśli to dopiero rozgrzewka, to kolejne spotkania zapowiadają się naprawdę interesująco. 

Link do następnego spotkania: https://community.sap.com/t5/katowice-events/katowice-sap-stammtisch-15-04-2026/ev-p/14328630

Pozdrawiamy serdecznie!

SAP Cpmmunity Katowice

entry.title: A more applied, role‑aligned certification for SAP S/4HANA Production Planning and Manufacturing
entry.content:
We’re excited to introduce the reimagined C_TS422 certification, designed to help you gain real, end-to-end SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing competence by turning knowledge into project readiness and measurable operational impact.

The updated format better reflects day‑to‑day project realities and validates practical capability in SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing. The certification remains relevant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA on‑premise.

Why it matters
C_TS422 now emphasizes applied competence in realistic planning and manufacturing contexts, making it easier to identify professionals who can deliver outcomes. For consultants, it showcases the capabilities that matter on the ground: sound decision-making, understanding dependencies, and executing processes end to end.

What’s new
The exam is built around how work actually happens. It prioritizes using knowledge in authentic scenarios rather than recalling isolated facts, aligns to real consultant tasks across projects and operations, and expects an end-to-end view of how configuration choices, master data, planning and execution steps fit together and affect upstream and downstream processes.

What stays the same
The core domains and overall scope are unchanged. You’ll still be assessed on the fundamentals and key processes of Production Planning and Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition. The certification continues to verify the core skills required for the SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing consultant profile and readiness to contribute in a mentored project role.

What you’ll learn and prove
Beyond topic knowledge, the certification validates how you translate requirements into working solutions. Candidates demonstrate application configuration, capture as-is processes and design to-be processes, blueprint effectively, and support change management. It also recognizes practical enablers of success through structured knowledge transfer, workshop facilitation, and Line-of-Business process expertise that drives adoption.


The certification covers the processes central to planning and manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA: Manufacturing Master Data Management, Production Planning, Capacity Planning, Managing Production Orders, Product Management, and Shop Floor Control. It reflects how functional design and master data decisions translate into reliable execution.

C_TS422 keeps trusted content but modernizes the way competence is measured. It highlights practical, role-aligned capabilities across planning and manufacturing, giving customers and consultants a more meaningful signal of project readiness and operational impact.

Get certified now: https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-sap-s-4hana-production-planning-and-manufacturing

entry.title: SAP CodeJAM Cali (Colombia)
entry.content:
Hi @thomas_jung as part of my promise to @ajmaradiaga We will be really proud and exited to host a couple of SAP Code JAM sessions in Globant's Cali office:

 

  • Location
    Cali Colombia.  Globant's Office.   It's a safe neighbourhood, close to mall, restaurants and hotels.  We have available "the training room" and we admits externals without any issue.

 

  • Requested Topic if you have a preference
    • Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding
    • ABAP Cloud & ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
    • Foundations of AI applications with the SAP HANA Cloud: ML, Vectors, Knowledge Graph
    •  
  • Time frame if you have something in mind
    • August 2026  16.08.2026 - 20.08.2026 or 26.08.2026 - 30.08.2026 (I'm excluding weeks with national holidays)
    • September 2026 any week will be Ok

Hoping you guys can come and visit Cali Colombia.

See you!!

entry.title: Re: KE30.
entry.content: Thank you so much for your explanation. Regarding the first point, the FI postings are assigned to the correct value fields. Regarding the second point you mentioned, I'm working with SAP ECC, and I don't know where to check if the 'FI/MM quotations' include a profitability segment. Regarding point 3, I don't know where to check if the cost elements assigned to the cost groups are correctly linked to the planned value fields. I like the KEAT transaction because I was able to see the general ledger accounts associated with the cost of goods sold.

entry.title: Archiving strategy for CDS-based AFO reports in S/4HANA
entry.content:
In our ECC landscape, we have  implemented an archiving solution using a custom utility class and ZARIX tables based on Info Structures to support reporting and meet performance requirements.

With the transition to S/4HANA, our reporting is now based on Analysis for Office (AFO) using CDS views. 

What approaches are you using for archiving or data aging for data consumed by CDS views? How do you enable access to historical or archived data in AFO reports within S/4HANA?

We would greatly appreciate insights and experiences from the community regarding practical and scalable solutions in S/4HANA environments.

Thank you in advance for sharing your knowledge and recommendations.

With regards,

Mrinal

entry.title: Re: KE30.
entry.content:
For your query 'I don't know where to check if the 'FI/MM quotations' include a profitability segment',

with the below transactions, you can check the profitability segment

FB03 (Display Document) → drill into line item → check the CO‑PA segment assignment.
KE24 (CO‑PA Line Item Display) → enter your document number → see if the posting created a CO‑PA line item.

If missing, the root cause would be Account assignment in FI/MM didn’t derive a PA segment (e.g., missing derivation rule in KE4I, or account not set up for CO‑PA transfer).

For your other query 'I don't know where to check if the cost elements assigned to the cost groups are correctly linked to the planned value fields'

check with the below transactions

KEI1 – Define PA Transfer Structure (cost element → value field mapping).
KEI2 – Assign PA Transfer Structure to operating concern.
KE4I – Assign accounts to PA transfer structure.

Verification Steps:

  1. Go into KEI1 and look at the transfer structure you’re using.
  2. Each cost element (or cost element group) should point to the intended value field.
  3. Cross‑check in KE30: the report reads only those value fields. If the mapping is wrong, the values won’t appear.

Tip: Use KEAT (Reconciliation) to see which G/L accounts are mapped and whether they’re flowing into CO‑PA. This is the best way to confirm alignment.

entry.title: Re: SAP LGM problem with mobile applications

entry.title: Re: Devolução de Transferência
entry.content: Ola! Eu estou configurando um processo de Devolução de Transferência entre empresas do mesmo Grupo, seguindo a SAPnote 2453291. O processo de envio deu certo, o processo de devolução, quando vou fazer a VL10B para criar a Delivery, ele não considera o tipo de documento de vendas que criei cópia do DLR, ele insiste em considerar o DLR. Já conferi várias vezes a configuração e na teoria não faz sentido. Foi criado documento novos com base na SAP NOTE, limpeza de controles de cópia no que é necessário, etc. O único que se mantém o mesmo, é o Pedido de Compras criado cópia do NB, que atua tanto na saída quando na criação do documento de Devolução, tendo somente a diferença do flag de devolução no pedido, que faz com que ele traga o documento de remessa que criei para devolução e todos os outros documentos atribuídos à ele. Alguém conseguiu fazer este processo e se teve este mesmo problema, como resolveu?

entry.title: Re: SAP LGM problem with mobile applications
entry.content:
My assumption is that there is some basic setup missing in your system. May I ask you to please create a support ticket so we can have a look at your system via a support user: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_LOGISTICS_MANAGEMENT/b91ea6cecd6d415f9b9f662c69ad651a/55537d5c36e9491e955fa8b54409a26c.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

Please use the following component for the support ticket: SCM-LGM-MOB

entry.title: Re: Achieve Event Driven Architecture and FIFO Approach with Google PubSub Adapter in Cloud Integrat
entry.content:
@KaranRajwar 

Very well written blog regarding Google Pub Sub adapter.

However, I have to disagree with the premise that 'majority' of EDA revolves around FIFO. You are mixing Business Logic Validation with Message Ordering.

Your examples of 'Payment -> Shipping' and 'Order Processed -> Inventory' are Logical Dependencies, not FIFO use cases. In a decoupled architecture, Inventory service simply reacts to OrderProcessed trigger. If that trigger hasn't fired, the inventory doesn't change. Shipping service reacts to PaymentProcessed trigger or validation/gatekeeper in place: is Payment processed ? etc

Real FIFO use cases are much narrower - they are reserved for State Race Conditions within the same entity. For example, ensuring OrderCancelled event doesn't 'leapfrog' OrderCreated event during network retry or system backlog. That is actual lifecycle problem.

Using FIFO unnecessarily is overkill that turns an asynchronous system back into a slow, synchronous one. Even if you are very lucky and your Event Broker enables Partitions like Kafka or AEM/Solace, FIFO is still highly ops demanding and should be minimized at all times

Second aspect, worth to mention is that to establish FIFO, you need all parties (sender and receiver) to ensure FIFO, middleware is not enough.

br
Mateusz

entry.title: Re: SAP LGM problem with mobile applications
entry.content: Please create a support ticket via https://launchpad.support.sap.com/ so we can track your case and assign it to the respective expert. For the system access itself, please additionally follow the guide to create a support user. Without this support user we are not able to access your system.

entry.title: Re: Structured address data elements in ISO 20022 messages
entry.content:
@KHummel Dear Klaus,

our approach is to by default display only structured addresses in V9 trees. As you correctly mentioned, AdrLine nodes are still available, only deactivated. So if you require also the AdrLine nodes, you can simply copy one of the standard DMEEX trees and reactivate those nodes.

Same approach applies if you want to hide some of the active nodes.

Kind regards,

Ondrej

entry.title: Re: STO Retorno - Transferência na localização Brasil
entry.content:
Olá Patrícia e Pessoal!

Estou configurando o processo de Devolução de transferência entre empresas do mesmo grupo, mercadoria em trânsito. Estou seguindo a orientação sap pnotes 2453291

Fiz a configuração conforme orientação e estou tendo problema no momento de criar a VL10B da Devolução, onde insiste em ter como referência o tipo de ordem de venda DLR do standard ao invés de buscar o tipo de ordem de vendas que criei cópia do DLR e do qual tem toda a atribuição realizada em todos os documentos que foram criados, conforme orientação da SAP Notes. Se eu coloco no controle de cópia VTLA o DLR para o documento de remessa cópia do NCR, ele cria a remessa de devolução, porém, me dá problema na ABA Brasil onde traz toda a parte de dispositivo lega, código de impostos e CFOP. No debug vimos que ele busca o NFTYPE, que no caso estou considerando o que tenho criado de devolução, mas ele busca o DLR, que não foi configurado aqui. Alguém já passou por isso? Ou conseguiu fazer funcionar este processo?

entry.title: GSTR6 - Not able to submit the report in "Run Statutory Reports" app
entry.content:
GSTR6 - Not able to submit the report in "Run Statutory Reports" app


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: GSTR6 - Not able to submit the report in "Run Statuto...
entry.content:
Dear all,

The GSTR-6 report provides details of all documents issued for the distribution of input tax credit, supplier invoices received by the ISD unit, and the manner in which the credit is distributed, including the related tax invoices. This monthly return must be filed by every Input Service Distributor (ISD). It contains information about the Input Tax Credit (ITC) received by the ISD and its subsequent distribution. Filing GSTR-6 is mandatory for all ISDs, even if there are no transactions (Nil return).

 

The source document types and the relevant data required for GSTR6A consistency checks have been defined. These will also be treated as source types for GSTR6 reporting purposes. Please refer the below help document to configuration for key users,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/d977d2cab26c45f2aa0d26a749955629.html?version=LATEST&locale=en-US

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Re: Consignment process and Sales of Non-Stock Item with...
entry.content:
Hi Lakshmipathi,

Thanks for the response.

Regarding Option 1, When we procure from the external supplier, system is allocating the inventory to the sales order(Ex: 1000) from the BDN process. So when i create consignment fill-up order(Ex:2000), system is not allowing to pick the inventory(Order 1000) which is from BDN process.

I think the limitation could be system cannot handle 2 special stock from a transaction(In this case, consignment stock and sales order stock)

Regarding Option 2,  I checked and it seems we cannot change or create new schedule line categories.

Let me further investigate on this.

With regards

Vinu Nanjan

entry.title: Re: Instruction Key Mapping to Payment Media File
entry.content:
Dear @thomas_janssen ,

If you are on S/4 HANA you have the fields CODE1-4, TEXT1-4 and ADD_INFO1-4 in the structure FPAYHX that read the attributes of particular Instructions. If you are on ECC, you need to read them yourself.

If we are speaking about our CGI payments formats, we already do read Instructions also in our solution for ECC. We use Instruction 1 for Service Level Code, Instruction 2 for Category Purpose Code and Instruction 3 for Local Instrument Code. The logic for Local Instrument Code is rather new, so you may need to download the latest notes as per our collective CGI notes depending on your system version:

S/4: https://me.sap.com/notes/2784858/E

ECC: https://me.sap.com/notes/2253571/E

Kind regards,

Ondrej

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation Inputs no longer visible...
entry.content:
Hi @MassimoC @Slowbie @BinlaThipsukhum @DavidRSG @romapi and other people facing the forementioned issue,

Just to let you know support's come back to me to let me know the fix has been deployed. I've just tested and indeed situation seems to be back to normal:

You should no longer be facing this issue as well.

BR,

LM.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Hello from Chicago!

entry.title: Re: Issues with the new C_S4CFI
entry.content: Hello Diana, thank you very much for your support. My attempt was regranted and I was able to take it successfully within the 24h timeframe of the still "active" activity. My case with the support is still open and I wonder, how the support is not aware of the issues and are still telling me that I should clear my browser cache. I have copy-pasted your response into the ticket, so that other cases can be handled more efficiently.

entry.title: Re: How to reprocess the full language installation ?
entry.content:
Hi Frederic, 

did you checked 

195442 - Language import and Support Packages 

352941 - Consultation: Languages and Support Packages

 

Importing a patch after language supplementation

If you have supplemented a language and then imported Support Packages, there is a risk of supplemented texts being deleted again. This especially affects languages that are rarely translated.
For this reason we recommend you repeat the language supplementation after you have imported a Support Package. A language supplementation in client 000 (client-independent tables) should be sufficient.

 

in your screenshot is shown that your supplementation language (english) has some warnings (yellow triangle).

furthermore, did you observe this behaviour in every client? 

you can also check in i18n whats your secondary language

 

best regards

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation Inputs no longer visible...
entry.content: I can confirm, the fix is working for me as well. Thank you for the continuous follow up, that really helped

entry.title: Re: Material showing in missing part report for productio...
entry.content: So the process is there are 2 Stocks available for component. Once Production order is released the component is confirmed and shows as reserved. sales order was created after that , we have a nightly ATP job which reschedule the confirmed quantity from production order and assign it to the sales order. now the Production order shows missing parts. i have checked the config for the ATP rules for sales order and the Checkbox for reservation is marked so it should not consume the stocks from reservation. we have not activated the Advanced ATP and are in S4 hana on premise.

entry.title: When do I use the generic Event "Data Change" and when more specific Events such as "Promotion"?
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

In Employee Central, when do I use the generic Event "Data Change" and when the more specific Events "Promotion", "Transfer", "Pay Rate Change", etc., which also imply changes to job and compensation information?

Thanks for your support,
Elina

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
@mageshwaran_subramanian great to see you in the community. 
May I ask your topics of interest / expertise?
Have you thought about running a community event? Please learn more about this great opportunity to build local engagement. Become an Event Organizer | SAP Community

Tagging @lauraschmitz too.

Best of luck!

entry.title: Re: SAC - Passing URL Variable Parameters to more than on...
entry.content:
you have to find the model id prefix. the easiest way is to write a script like =>

var modelInfo = Tablle_1.getDataSource().getInfo();

var myModelId = modelInfo.modelId;

Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info,  myModelId);

 

you will get something like => t.MY_CONN:C6661pg5rsjqpfbbb1qmmm051j

then using your prefix  you can create the URL=> 

https://<TENANT_URL>/sap/fpa/ui/app.html#/analyticapp?shellMode=embed&/aa/<APP_ID>/?v01Model=t.MY_CONN:m1n2o3p4q5r6s7t8u9v0&v01Par=Z_VAR_BRANCH_MAIN&v01Val=5000&v02Model=t.MY_CONN:a9b8c7d6e5f4g3h2i1j0&v02Par=Z_VAR_BRANCH_MAIN&v02Val=4500

entry.title: Re: GOS Attachment in RAP Object Page
entry.content:
Hello,

I think it is possible via Fiori extensions. You can use attachment service library ("sap.se.mi.plm.lib.attachmentservice"). Here it is described but it is different case. You need to configure it to the standard Purchase Order business object.

Attachment Service to your rescue in S4HANA via Fi... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: CDHDR table performance issue.
entry.content:
Hi Subbaka11!

Are you already on a HANA database? If you are, you could try with JOINING CDHDR with CDPOS and restrict the selected data based on for example the table you are interested in. We have quite a few such selects in our Z-code and it's working fine.

Hope this helps!

Bärbel

entry.title: Partnershap
entry.content:
Hi ,

Please permit me to join .

entry.title: Re: Error 8B223 – Nota Fiscal existente para el proveedor
entry.content:
Hola, Ya encontré respuesta sobre este caso, se las comparto porque al parecer es algo que sucede de forma recurrente.

Revisé el caso en detalle y validándolo con consultores de Brasil, aclaro que el comportamiento que se está presentando al momento de facturar, es correcto

En Brasil por requerimientos legales de trazabilidad fiscal, SAP no permite contabilizar más de una MIRO & VIM = Nota Fiscal para una misma MIGO (Recepción).
Este control es estándar del sistema y no es configurable, ya que responde a la legislación brasileña. Lo que sí está permitido es el siguiente escenario:

Una sola Nota Fiscal puede estar asociada a varias recepciones de la misma o distintas OCs.

Lo que no está permitido:

Varias Notas Fiscales (MIRO) asociadas a una única recepción (MIGO).
En este caso, el sistema emite el error:

“Nota Fiscal ya creada para la recepción de mercancías”

Por lo tanto, para que el proceso funcione correctamente en Brasil, la forma correcta de operar es:

Si el proveedor emite una sola Nota Fiscal, se pueden realizar recepciones parciales (varias MIGO) y luego contabilizar una única MIRO que tome todas esas recepciones.

Si el proveedor emite varias Notas Fiscales, entonces se deben realizar recepciones separadas (una MIGO por cada factura) y luego contabilizar una MIRO & VIM por cada MIGO, utilizando siempre números de Nota Fiscal distintos.

Desde el punto de vista de SAP, el sistema está funcionando según lo esperado.
Lo que sería necesario revisar es el proceso de negocio, ya que en Brasil no es válido registrar una sola recepción para luego facturarla en varias Notas Fiscales.

Espero haber sido clara. Gracias

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: @StephanieMarley  Thank you!. I participate and contribute in Master data related topics.

entry.title: Re: Granting OSS access to new S-user ID for maintenance...
entry.content: hi @Carly_Scarpitti  if you want the long story, ggl "how to grant a user to create oss incident from solution manager" . The short story is grant necessary roles and link the s-user to the solman user in AISUSER. cheers!A

entry.title: Re: CAP-consuming on-premise OData API : read ECONNRESET...
entry.content:
I also adding this `ui5.yaml` proxy configuration fixed the **ECONNRESET** error in my UI5 app 

**What was happening before**

My app was calling the backend directly from the browser, for example: * https://approuter-test.../api-je * http://127.0.0.1:3050 This caused classic connection issues: * CORS restrictions * HTTPS → HTTP mixed content blocking * SSL certificate validation problems * BAS / browser security policies The browser tried to connect directly and the connection was being closed, resulting in `ECONNRESET`. ---

**What changed with ui5-middleware-simpleproxy**

After adding the proxy middleware, the flow became: Browser → UI5 Dev Server → Proxy → Real Backend Instead of calling the backend directly, the UI now calls local paths like: ``` /api /api-je ``` The UI5 dev server forwards the request to the real endpoint behind the scenes. This solves multiple problems at once: * The browser sees requests as same-origin → no CORS issues * No mixed-content errors (HTTPS frontend can reach HTTP backend) * SSL validation is handled by the middleware instead of the browser * Network restrictions from BAS or CF are avoided ---

**Key parts that made it work** *

`mountPath` creates a local endpoint that the UI calls * `beforeMiddleware: serveResources` ensures requests are intercepted early * `strictSSL: false` allows local HTTP or self-signed certs without connection resets Example: ``` fetch("/api") // works fetch("http://127.0.0.1:3050") // caused ECONNRESET before ``` ---

**Short explanation** The ECONNRESET disappeared because the browser stopped calling the backend directly. The UI5 dev server proxy now handles the connection, avoiding CORS, SSL, and mixed-content issues.

entry.title: Re: Programmatically retrieving lineage/source object inf...
entry.content: Hi @CLTGravesen, Thank you very much for taking the time to write such a detailed explanation, it really helped me move in the right direction. 🙂 Based on your suggestion about using the CLI READ OBJECT output, I extracted the CSN file for my Models and was actually able to trace a field back to its originating Datasphere view and further down to the source table by programmatically parsing the dependency tree in the CSN. It definitely required some effort to interpret the structure, but your hint that the dependency information exists there was the key insight I was looking for. Really appreciate you sharing your experience and pointing me toward the CLI approach. Thanks again for your help! Best, Shubham

entry.title: Re: Salesforce Adapter download option not found
entry.content:
Adapter Installation on Cloud Foundry can be done following below steps.

Same is available in the documentation - https://api.sap.com/package/SalesforceAdapter/documents - Salesforce Adapter and Plug-in Installation Guide 

1. Navigate to the Discover tab.

2. Using the search box, search for “Salesforce Adapter for SAP Integration Suite”.

3. Select the “Salesforce Adapter for SAP Integration Suite” package.

4. Navigate to the Copy option. Click Copy to replicate the package from the Discover workspace to the Design workspace.

5. Navigate to the Design workspace and select the Salesforce Adapter for SAP Integration Suite package.

6. Select Artifacts tab and choose the Deploy action to deploy the Salesforce Adapter.

entry.title: Re: Integrating low code solutions with Microsoft using SAP Integration Suite has never been easier!
entry.content:
Hello Martin,

Again thanks for your blog. It assumes that we are receiving from EntraId a JWT token but it the sender receives an SAML assertion from EntraId instead of the JWT token how will be the process of Principal Propagation? easier right? because the token exchange wouldnt be necessary?

Thanks

Jorge

entry.title: Maintaining of Usage attribute
entry.content: Is there a different way on maintaining the Usage Attribute in the Packaging Data based on the Company Role. For Example for producers selling e.g. produced packaging to another company who is using it to package their products. Would the input be commercial since they aren't selling it to the end-consumer or would it be houshould because it will eventually end up with the end-consumer?

entry.title: Re: How to properly use BAdI SD_SLS_DATA_TRANSFER_HEAD?
entry.content:
Hello @bigflatfoot

First, you need to configure copy routine. 

--> go to app Manage Your Solution --> 103089

--> Create routine 

--> Assign enhancement id

once that is done, you can implement this badi in Custom logic and set up filter as Enchancement_ID = QUOTE. 

best regards, 

Freskita

entry.title: Re: Integrating low code solutions with Microsoft using SAP Integration Suite has never been easier!
entry.content:
Hey @JorgeCalderon,

the flow requires a SAML assertion to be passed to the SAP OAuth server for exchange. This article describes how to get a trusted SAML assertion issued by Entra ID for any known client app that wants to talk to SAP. If you generate your own SAML assertion you obtain the responsibility of an IDP like Entra yourself with all the risks that come with it.

The flow can not be shortened without compromising security.

Let the community know what you decided and why in the end 🙂

entry.title: Re: An End-to-End Hands-On Tutorial for Clean Core RAP Development
entry.content:
Hello Iulia, thanks for your fantastic work!

I have a problem in step 4 (create behavior definition), i retrieve error in line:

"create ( authorization : global );"

with message: "none" was expected, not "global". 

Any ideas about how solve this?

 

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Record quantity indicator in center cost
entry.content:
Hi @Chris1973 after reviewing the documentation, I still don't see any changes in the quantity field for accounts 6, which are expense accounts... I ran the test with the check active for both the cost center and the account.

The test I performed is as follows: I entered goods through the MIGO application for a stock order, and I see the quantity field reflected in account 3, which is stock with operation type BSX, but I don't see it in the expense and stock variation accounts with operation types EIN and EKG, which is where I want to see it. (The quantity record field is activated in both the expense account and the inventory variation account and and in both cost centers). I am attaching a screenshot of my view of the item with the quantity field.

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: SCC- Mudança na Gestão contributiva S.S. / Change in the contributory management of Social Secur
entry.content:
Good afternoon @Anabela_Lucio 
Hope you are well.

Are there any updates on this subject?

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Bin sorting in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @ST24,

In Public Cloud WM you can mass-upload / maintain bin sorting via the Migration Cockpit migration object Warehouse storage bin sorting. SAP explicitly states that updating existing bin sorting data is supported (file upload) and also mentions the practical limitation that you typically need a new migration project for repeated uploads of the same key combination

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/c5adf62e525b488290a6df8f4dd7da8a.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/61fa9952db084ac2a785160cc5c5191e.html

IMPORTANT: Those WM/EWM migration objects might be missing in Migration Cockpit unless the right scope is active. SAP Note lists Warehouse storage bin sorting among the WM objects and explains the symptom

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2884164

So, yes upload is possible in Public Cloud just not via the old classic EWM but via Migration Cockpit

How do you get sorting

SAP confirms the key behavior:

  • In Public Cloud WM a default bin sorting record is generated for each newly created storage bin
  • Then the migration object Warehouse storage bin sorting is available to update that generated default sorting data

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/c5adf62e525b488290a6df8f4dd7da8a.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/61fa9952db084ac2a785160cc5c5191e.html

Use the Sort Storage Bins app (/SCWM/SBST) to (re)generate the bin sorting for the relevant warehouse + activity + activity area

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('%252FSCWM%252FSBST')/S36

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/b7f2906dc5b94711aa7f139c4105c17b.html

SAP even provides in Note how to sequence for this topic:

  1. Go to customizing: Assign Storage Bins to Activity Areas
  2. Got to customizing: Define Sort Sequence for Activity Area
  3. Run Sort Storage Bins app (/SCWM/SBST)
  4. Verify in Display Storage Bin app

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3590140

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3676710

Additional information:

For mass bin creation you can use:

  • Create Multiple Storage Bins and SAP WM overview also lists Mass Create / Mass Edit Storage Bins type apps as available capabilities in this area

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/9980c8e010a3459a9263e270c01cad7e.html

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('%252FSCWM%252FLS11')/S36

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('%25252FSCWM%25252FLS10')/S36

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification for Partners: An Overview of the Latest Changes - Including Q&A

thanks for your question. I don’t have a dedicated, partner‑specific blog post at hand right now, but the following resources explain the overall direction SAP Certification is taking towards 2026:

Best regards,
Christina

Pavan K

entry.title: SAP HANA System Replication Takeover and Client Connectivity
entry.content:
Documented Scope and Architectural Responsibilities


Introduction
This article summarizes publicly available SAP documentation and reflects the documented behavior of SAP HANA System Replication at the time of writing.

SAP HANA System Replication is widely used in productive landscapes to improve availability and to enable takeover to a secondary system.
In operational situations, however, a recurring observation can occur:

After a takeover, the secondary system assumes the primary database role, while application connectivity depends on the surrounding client access path.

This article does not provide configuration or implementation guidance.
Instead, it consolidates SAP documentation to clarify the documented scope of System Replication and the responsibilities described in related high-availability integration documentation.

The purpose is to align operational expectations with documented behavior.


Documented Purpose of System Replication
According to SAP documentation, SAP HANA System Replication continuously transfers database changes from a primary system to a secondary system and supports a role change (takeover).

SAP documentation describes that System Replication:

  • replicates database persistence (data and log changes)
  • enables takeover to a secondary system
  • preserves transactional consistency

Sources
SAP HANA System Replication Guide
https://help.sap.com/doc/c81e9406d08046c0a118c8bef71f6bdc/2.0.07/en-US/SAP_HANA_System_Replication_Guide_en.pdf
SAP Help Portal – System Replication Overview
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/676844172c2442f0bf6c8b080db05ae7.html

After takeover, the secondary system becomes the new primary database system from the database perspective.
The documentation therefore describes System Replication primarily in terms of database availability and data consistency.


Documented Behavior During Takeover
During a takeover, SAP HANA changes the database role of the participating systems and starts the required database services on the new primary host.

From the database perspective described in the System Replication documentation, the takeover is considered completed once the primary role is active and database services are operational on the new primary host according to the System Replication documentation.

The System Replication Guide documents the database role change and service startup behavior.
The documentation describes database state and service availability, while network routing and client access handling are described separately in high-availability integration documentation.


Actions After Takeover in HA Integration Documentation
SAP documentation describes integration mechanisms that allow external components to react to a role change.

SAP HANA provides a HA/DR provider hook that enables external high-availability components to execute actions after a takeover.

Documented examples include service endpoint switching or cluster-related actions.

Sources
Implementing SAP HANA HA/DR Providers
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/1200ab8ef0c54c54be2d0e7f5327f7ed.html
Installing and Configuring HA/DR Provider Hook
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/6b94445c94ae495c83a19646e7c3fd56/2962efcfdd6740689d5705bdabe9a2d5.html

The HA/DR integration documentation describes coordination with external high-availability mechanisms following a database role change.


Database Role and Client Connectivity in Documented Architecture
System Replication changes the database role.
Client connectivity depends on the access path used by the application.

Typical productive environments use infrastructure components such as:

  • virtual IP addresses
  • cluster frameworks
  • load balancers
  • DNS-based host names

SAP System Replication documentation describes the database role and persistence synchronization, while HA integration documentation describes coordination with external availability mechanisms.
These documents therefore address different parts of the overall availability architecture.


Interpreting Connection Failures After Takeover
In operational situations, a takeover may be completed from the database perspective while application connections still fail if the client access path still targets the former primary host or has not yet been updated by the surrounding infrastructure.

From a documentation perspective, this reflects different layers of the availability architecture rather than a single mechanism.

SAP documentation separates:

  • database role change (System Replication)
  • external availability coordination (HA integration)

Reference
SAP Note 1999880 - FAQ: SAP HANA System Replication


Relationship to High Availability Architecture
SAP HANA high availability consists of multiple documented components, including:

  • System Replication
  • cluster or HA integration
  • infrastructure routing mechanisms

System Replication addresses database availability.
Other components coordinate access paths and service endpoints in the surrounding infrastructure.


Conclusion
SAP HANA System Replication provides database availability and transactional consistency after a takeover as described in the official documentation.

Client connectivity depends on surrounding high-availability and infrastructure mechanisms that are documented separately from System Replication.

Understanding the documented scope of these components helps distinguish database state from client access routing and supports clearer troubleshooting during incidents.

For implementation or configuration guidance, always follow the official SAP documentation and SAP recommendations.


Disclaimer
This article summarizes publicly available SAP documentation and does not replace official SAP configuration, sizing, or implementation guidance. This article describes documented architectural behavior and is not an SAP support statement.

entry.title: IBOP – Strategic Power of Unrestricted Checks, Confirmation Protection, Rounding and More!
entry.content:
If you haven't already, have a look at the first blog to understand the foundation of Interactive Backorder Processing (IBOP) before diving into the massive leap forward we've taken with the 2025 release series.

Scaling Interactive Backorder Processing in the 2025 Series

When the Manage Confirmations for Backorders Fiori app first launched with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS03, it revolutionized the "Order Fulfillment Specialist" role. It moved us away from static, scheduled automation toward a dynamic, real-time workspace where you could manually redistribute confirmations using strategies like Improve, Redistribute, or Lose.

While the 2023 release (FPS03) established the foundation for manual control + mass recheck, the 2025 release series shifts from simple adjustment to strategic command and informed decision making.

2025 FPS0: The "Superpower" of Unrestricted Checks

The 2025 FPS0 release introduces a feature that completely redefines the boundaries of aATP: Unrestricted Checks (Override Capabilities).

Breaking the Constraints

In standard ATP checks, the system acts as a strict gatekeeper, ensuring you never promise more than what is logically available. However, critical business moments — like securing a massive order for a VIP customer — demand the power to override those rules. Authorized users (via the CA_ATP_IAB authorization object) can now selectively bypass the following gatekeepers:

  • Sales Product Allocation (Sales PAL )
  • Product Availability Check (PAC) and Supply Protection (SUP)
  • Capacity Product Allocation (Capacity PAL)

Mass Recheck:

 

Manual Change:

How powerful is this?

Imagine a scenario where you have only 100 units of unrestricted stock, but a VIP customer needs 500 units. A normal check would fail for the additional 400 units. In Unrestricted Mode, you can bypass PAC and PAL constraints to grant a full confirmation of 500 units, even if it results in over-consumption (negative stock). This gives you the ultimate authority to prioritize critical business commitments over standard system limits.

The availability constraint status is depicted below: 

Transparency and Governance

With great power comes the need for traceability. It is crucial to highlight that once you publish these changes, any requirements checked as unrestricted against basic methods are logged in the Monitor BOP Application. This ensures that every strategic "override" is fully visible and auditable within the system's history.

Note: Logging in BOP Monitor is available only from 2025FPS1 release onwards.

2025 FPS0: Securing Your Decisions: Fixed Date and Quantity

To prevent your manual heroics from being overturned by the next automated Backorder Processing (BOP) run, 2025 FPS0 introduces the Set Fixed Date and Quantity option. Once "fixed," the system applies a "Skip" strategy during subsequent BOP runs, ensuring your manual commitments remain untouched.

To set the flag on an ATP-checked requirement:

Similarly, to reset:

2025 FPS1: Intelligence, Precision, and Harmony

Moving into 2025 FPS1, you can experience sophisticated behavior with System Delivery Proposals and full support for Rounding Profiles, ensuring your manual edits are both free handed (i.e, the system does not revert the confirmation to it's previous state in case the request cannot be met) and precisely rounded to the nearest quantity.

  • Intelligent Delivery Proposals: Speed is essential in high-volume environments. By enabling the "Accept system delivery proposal?" feature in your App Settings, the system will automatically propose confirmations based on the real-time stock situation during manual updates, drastically reducing the manual data entry and clicks needed to finalize your worklist.

  • Respecting Rounding Profiles: For businesses with strict packaging units, the system now fully respects Rounding Profiles. Any manual confirmation change is automatically adjusted to align with your maintained rounding configurations, ensuring technical accuracy.

Looking ahead, the future of Fulfillment is Interactive!

The evolution from 2023 FPS03 to the 2025 FPS1 release showcases a clear trajectory: providing users with immediate, interactive control without sacrificing the underlying governance of real-time ATP. Whether you are overriding restrictions to save a VIP relationship or using correlation groups to synchronize a complex shipment, IBOP ensures that your supply chain remains flexible, transparent, and most importantly, responsive to the speed of business.

For more information, please refer to:

  • 2025FPS0
  • 2025FPS1

Watch this space for more series of exciting features in the upcoming release. Stay tuned!

Kind regards,
Shishira Shastri H

entry.title: SAP Community CPH Event Plan - 2026
entry.content:
SAP Community Copenhagen – Event Plan for 2026

We are excited to share our SAP Community plan for 2026!

This year we are aiming at organizing at least four events – one per quarter – combining professional inspiration with cozy community social time.

In Q1, before Easter, we will do a social meet-up. This will be a great opportunity to reconnect, meet new members, and strengthen our community. The date will be the 26th of March and the event for sign up will be shared shortly.

In Q2, at the end of May after the SAPPHIRE conference in Madrid (19-21 May), we will host an event where we would like to invite 1–2 speakers from Denmark or abroad to share key announcements and highlights from SAPPHIRE. We are aiming at the 28th of May for this event.

After the summer holidays, in Q3, we will meet again for a social event while the weather is still good – a perfect setting for socializing and sharing our holiday pictures.

Finally, after SAP TechEd in the fourth quarter, we are planning a half-day or full-day event with inspirational talks from speakers from Denmark and abroad, focusing on the latest trends and insights. 

The dates for the second half of 2026 are still TBD. 

On behalf of the SAP Community CPH organizers, we are really looking forward to an inspiring year together and to growing our SAP Community even stronger in 2026!

Juan, Prakash, Pallavi, Christian, Asier

entry.title: Re: SAP Community CPH Event Plan - 2026
entry.content: Looking forward

entry.title: Re: Transport Request Error Object TVDIR,TDDAT cannot be locked
entry.content: hi @De81 Devyani Patil , first and foremost, that code looks to me a warning and not an error. And in regard to the message, it comes to my mind that you may have hit the moment someone else was releasing a transport OR a programmer forced in the transport request some T*DIR entries. Lastly, if the transport got released you may be good BUT you should check what entries are getting exported in the transport request before importing it in target systems.

entry.title: Re: Custom Cube for Margin Analysis - Calculated Key Figure shows no data
entry.content:
Hello Koushik, thank you for your reply. The calculated measure is already blank in ADT preview.

Please have a look at the screenshot. The Preview shows single rows. On single row level the calculation is of course impossible. It needs to aggregate the rows first so that RevenueGlobalCurr and COGSGlobalCurr are summarized.

But ADT is not doing it.

 

I am using DefaultAggregation SUM in the Restricted Measure Cube

//// Restricted Measures ////
  
    @EndUserText.label: 'Revenue'
    @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'GlobalCurrency'
    @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
    case when SemanticTag = 'ZGS_C_PRIC'
    and Ledger = '0L'
    then curr_to_decfloat_amount ( AmountInGlobalCurrency )
    else null
    end as RevenueGlobalCurr,
    
  
    @EndUserText.label: 'COGS'
    @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'GlobalCurrency'
    @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
    case when SemanticTag = 'ZGS_L_PRIC'
    and Ledger = '0L'
    then curr_to_decfloat_amount ( AmountInGlobalCurrency )
    else null
    end as COGSGlobalCurr,

And also using it in the Calculcated Measure Cube

 

////  Key Figures with Semantic Tag

  @EndUserText.label: 'Revenue'
  @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'GlobalCurrency'
    RevenueGlobalCurr,
    
  @EndUserText.label: 'COGS'
  @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'GlobalCurrency'
    COGSGlobalCurr,

It looks like the aggregation is not working as intended? What can be done to fix it?

I also removed all dimensions that are not needed to allow aggregation but this also did not fix the issue.

Thank you

David

entry.title: Re: Split Freight Settlement Document by charge type currency
entry.content:
Hi Ajinkya,

Did your resolve with this specific "One carrier = One freight agreement but different charge-type currencies. "?
I cant customize scenario in SPRO with different charge levels (head, item).

entry.title: Re: SAP Community CPH Event Plan - 2026
entry.content: I hope I will be able to catch you all at one of the events!

entry.title: Re: Reading GOS(Generic Object Services) in MM03
entry.content: Hi @rmz01, you can use the same logic from the above post—specifically the ZREADGOS function module—where you pass the MATNR and its business object to retrieve all GOS attachments. Just go through the code and you’ll get the idea.

entry.title: Re: Service Entry Sheet Performance period is not defaulted
entry.content: Thanks David for your reply, We have a valid performance start and end date in PO. Still not showing by default in the SES on reference to PO. I had raised a SAP case in this regard.

entry.title: Re: Did you know this about Capacity Planning in S/4HANA?
entry.content:
Hello Jenn,

Thanks for the interesting and thorough article - not much to find elsewhere on this subject 🙂

Can you help us clarify the following to questions:

1) While activating LiveCache in a RISE setup is assumed free of charge, are there any App Usage fees/licenses to be considered for apps utilizing the LiveCache?

2) Are there any further performance impacts to consider when activating LiveCache, like increase of CPU/RAM usage?

Thank you.

Enterprise Architecture - RISE RISE with SAP

entry.title: Re: Why ABAP do not have JSON de/serializer in kernel?
entry.content:
CALL TRANSFORMATION is also executed in the kernel (Core). And yes, you are right, it knows ABAP data, in some way, and can even create data (add new lines to known tables), but can not generate ABAP types (structures). And it is extremely fast, compared to implementations of IF_JSON_READER or IF_SXML_READER, because it does not have multiple callbacks from the kernel to ABAP runtime. At the same time, it is not flexible because of the simplification and normalization. ABAP is not JavaScript. ABAP is not case sensitive - you need to support different name conversion rules from JS to ABAP. It has a limit on the component name length, but JSON does not. ABAP does not have a built-in boolean (already has, but this does not help with old code)  - JSON has. ABAP has conversion exits. ABAP has much more complex data type support compared to JSON (different numerics, different types of tables, date+time+timestamp), and for all these, you need bidirectional conversion. It all costs performance. If you ask for customization of the conversion (and it will be asked), it will also affect performance. And at the end, you will not be much faster than a good implementation on top of IF_JSON_READER/IF_JSON_WRITER. 

But you may try with your initiative, but just be more precise and ask for something like CALL TRANSFORMATION id, but more flexible. 

I will also add my explanation and link to your CI request to my GitHub page to help people with similar requests 😉

P.S: I have already done a prototype of /ui2/cl_json with IF_JSON_READER/IF_JSON_WRITER - it may be ~40% faster, compared to ABAP only implementation, but some features, like type generation is quite difficult with this approach.
